steps.title: Identify the requirement for the claim
steps.content: The second team claims these sounds are primarily for intraspecies communication. For this claim to hold, other caterpillars must actually be present when sounds are produced—communication requires an audience.

steps.title: Test which finding most directly challenges this
steps.content:
Choice C — Caterpillars that were found to produce sounds in response to simulated attacks are typically solitary and were tested in isolation. reveals that the caterpillars producing these sounds are typically solitary and tested in isolation.

If no other caterpillars are present, intraspecies communication is physically impossible, directly destroying the second team's claim.

The defensive-function alternatives support a different explanation, while the species-without-sounds alternative does not address the sound-producing caterpillars.

That matches Choice C — Caterpillars that were found to produce sounds in response to simulated attacks are typically solitary and were tested in isolation..
